testcase.name: test_get_status_bellefield

failure.message: AssertionError: Lab(name='Thaw Hall M06', status=True, available_co[91 chars]s=29) != Lab(name='Bellefield 314', status=False, available_[93 chars]s=30)

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.lab_test.LabTest testMethod=test_get_status_bellefield>
@responses.activate def test_get_status_bellefield(self): responses.add( responses.GET, create_test_url("BELLEFIELD"), json=lab_mocks.mocked_bellefield_data, )
result = lab.get_one_lab_data("BELLEFIELD")
self.assertIsInstance(result, lab.Lab) > self.assertEqual( result, lab.Lab( name="Bellefield 314", status=False, available_computers=29, off_computers=1, in_use_computers=0, out_of_service_computers=0, total_computers=30, ), ) E AssertionError: Lab(name='Thaw Hall M06', status=True, available_co[91 chars]s=29) != Lab(name='Bellefield 314', status=False, available_[93 chars]s=30)
tests/lab_test.py:44: AssertionError

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.lab_test.LabTest testMethod=test_get_status_lawrence>
@responses.activate def test_get_status_lawrence(self): responses.add( responses.GET, create_test_url("LAWRENCE"), json=lab_mocks.mocked_lawrence_data, )
result = lab.get_one_lab_data("LAWRENCE")
self.assertIsInstance(result, lab.Lab) > self.assertEqual( result, lab.Lab( name="David Lawrence 230", status=False, available_computers=25, off_computers=10, in_use_computers=5, out_of_service_computers=0, total_computers=40, ), ) E AssertionError: Lab(n[28 chars]atus=True, available_computers=32, off_compute[68 chars]s=38) != Lab(n[28 chars]atus=False, available_computers=25, off_comput[70 chars]s=40)

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.lab_test.LabTest testMethod=test_get_status_sutherland>
@responses.activate def test_get_status_sutherland(self): responses.add( responses.GET, create_test_url("SUTH"), json=lab_mocks.mocked_sutherland_data, )
result = lab.get_one_lab_data("SUTH")
self.assertIsInstance(result, lab.Lab) > self.assertEqual( result, lab.Lab( name="Sutherland 120", status=False, available_computers=11, off_computers=1, in_use_computers=0, out_of_service_computers=0, total_computers=12, ), ) E AssertionError: Lab(n[51 chars]ters=6, off_computers=4, in_use_computers=1, o[41 chars]s=11) != Lab(n[51 chars]ters=11, off_computers=1, in_use_computers=0, [42 chars]s=12)

testcase.name: test_handle_invalid_lab_id
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.lab_test.LabTest testMethod=test_handle_invalid_lab_id>
@responses.activate def test_handle_invalid_lab_id(self): responses.add( responses.GET, create_test_url("CATH_G27"), body="Resource not found", status=404, )
> with pytest.raises( lab.LabAPIError, match="The Lab ID was invalid. Please open a GitHub issue so we can resolve this.", ): E Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'pittapi.lab.LabAPIError'>
tests/lab_test.py:234: Failed

testcase.name: test_handle_unexpected_fetch_err
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.lab_test.LabTest testMethod=test_handle_unexpected_fetch_err>
@responses.activate def test_handle_unexpected_fetch_err(self): responses.add( responses.GET, create_test_url("CATH_G27"), body="Unauthorized", status=401, )
> with pytest.raises( lab.LabAPIError, match="An unexpected error occurred while fetching lab data: Unauthorized", ): E Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'pittapi.lab.LabAPIError'>
